summary: COMPARISON two unit Squares. LEFT 'Independent' (GREEN caption): BLUE B column with GREEN A∩B and A∩B^c both of equal height 0.6 — P(A|B) = P(A|B^c). RIGHT 'Dependent' (RED caption): same BLUE column but A∩B height 0.85 vs A∩B^c height 0.25 — the green region's height jumps across the B boundary. Bottom caption states the independence rule P(A|B)=P(A|B^c) and its negation for the dependent panel.
